#  Manage Insurance Documents and Compliance Status on Commitments with the Directory Tools 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)
  * **Additional Information** :
    * To learn how to manage contract compliance documents and statuses, see [Manage Contract Compliance Documents & Statuses for a Commitment](https://support.procore.com/products/online/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-contract-compliance-documents-and-statuses-for-a-commitment).


##  Prerequisites 
  * To add the 'Compliance' tab to a commitment, Procore Pay must be enabled on the project. See [Enable or Disable Procore Pay on Your Projects](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-payment-processing-tab-in-the-payments-tool/enable-or-disable-pay).
  * To track insurance compliance on commitments with Procore Pay, the 'Insurance Compliant' payment requirement for Procore Pay must be turned ON. See [Configure Payment Requirements: Commitment Requirements](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/configure-payment-requirements).
  * To add insurance records to one or both of Procore's Directory tools so you can add them to a project's commitments, see:
    * [Add Insurance to a Company Record in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-insurance-to-a-company-record-in-the-company-directory)
    * [Add Project Insurance to a Company Record in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-project-insurance-to-a-company-in-the-project-directory)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the Project level **Commitments** tool.
  2. Under the **Contracts** tab, locate the commitment to work with.
  3. Click the **Number** link to open it.
  4. In the commitment, click the **Compliance** tab.
  5. Scroll to the **Insurance** card.
  6. Choose from these options: 
#####  Notes
     * When managing insurance information with the Directory tools, actions are always logged in the Change History tab of the corresponding Directory tool instead of the commitment's Change History tab.
     * [Add Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/manage-insurance-documents--statuses)
     * [View Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/manage-insurance-documents--statuses)
     * [Edit Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/manage-insurance-documents--statuses)
     * [Remove Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/invoice-administrator-guide/manage-insurance-documents--statuses)


#### Add Insurance
To add new insurance information, choose the appropriate option:
#####  Note
The **Add Insurance** and **Edit** menu buttons are only visible and available to an [invoice administrator](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-invoice-administrators) who is also a designated Insurance Manager (see [Designate an Insurance Manager for Your Procore ](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/designate-an-insurance-manager-for-your-procore-company)[Company](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/designate-an-insurance-manager-for-your-procore-company)) or who has 'Admin' level permissions on the Company level Directory tool.  
| ![Insurance Card Add Insurance](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/1fb069a9f1f359b931d67688d2b6069d/033503c363aea66fed144cc7067200b7/insurance-card-add-insurance.png?fm=webp)  | ![Edit Insurance Button Label](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/293ac8911993ebbd815726555be0e952/59f9a1c281e5291a35abc46c2391b71c/edit-insurance-button-label.png?fm=webp)  |  
| --- | --- |  
After you choose a menu option, Procore launches the Company or Project level Directory tool and opens the company profile of the 'Contract Company' to the Insurance tab. You can then follow the steps in:
  * [Add Insurance to a Company Record in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-insurance-to-a-company-record-in-the-company-directory)
  * [Add Project Insurance to a Company Record in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-project-insurance-to-a-company-in-the-project-directory)


#### View Insurance
If the 'Contract Company' on the commitment has any existing insurance information stored in the company profile of the Directory tools, that insurance information appears as rows in the table in the commitment's **Insurance** card.
![Insurance Card With Edit Button](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/04d989af8e5256863aab608ad301bcf5/18ba987e8d13cee14d0da67acc8f04bd/insurance-card-with-edit-button.png?fm=webp)
##### Insurance Card
This table details the elements of the Insurance card in a commitment.  
| **Add Insurance** or **Edit**  | Menu Buttons  |  Users with the appropriate permissions can either click the **Add Insurance** button (if this is the first insurance entry) or the **Edit** button (if this is a subsequent insurance entry) and choose one (1) menu option:
  * **Company Insurance**. Launches the Company Directory and opens the company profile to the 'Insurance' tab so you can add, edit, or remove insurance information. See [Add Insurance to a Company Record in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-insurance-to-a-company-record-in-the-company-directory).
  * **Project Insurance**. Launches the Project Directory and opens the company profile to the 'Insurance' tab so you can add, edit, or remove insurance information. See [Add Project Insurance to a Company Record in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-project-insurance-to-a-company-in-the-project-directory).


#####  Note
The **Add Insurance** and **Edit** menu buttons are only visible and available to an [invoice administrator](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/add-invoice-administrators) who is also a designated Insurance Manager (see [Designate an Insurance Manager for Your Procore ](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/designate-an-insurance-manager-for-your-procore-company)[Company](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/designate-an-insurance-manager-for-your-procore-company)) or granted 'Admin' level permissions on the Company level Directory tool.  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| **Compliance Status**  | Status  |  Procore looks at the insurance information stored in the Directory tools to determine the commitment's status:
  * **Compliant**. Indicates all insurance information is in the 'Compliant' status.
  * **Not Compliant**. Indicates that at least one (1) insurance entry is set to a status other than 'Compliant' in the Directory tools. 
#####  Important
    * For this status to read 'Compliant', all listed insurance entries from the Directory tool(s) must also be in the 'Compliant' status.
    * If the insurance information from the Directory tools is set to any other available status (including the 'Compliant - In Progress' status), the status in the Commitments tool shows 'Not Compliant' to ensure that all insurance information is fully compliant before your team releases an invoice payment.

 |  
| **Name**  | Field  | Shows the name from the Company or Project Directory. Typically, users enter the name of the insurance certificate, policy, or document in this field.  |  
| **Level**  | Field  | Shows whether the insurance information was added to the Company or Project Directory.  |  
| **Effective Date**  | Field  | Shows the effective date for the insurance information in the Company or Project Directory.  |  
| **Expiration Date**  | Field  |  Shows the expiration date from the Company or Project Directory. These icons also alert users when the insurance is nearing or past the expiration date:  ![Expiring Soon](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/ffb03c299fc5e37e7fd1351993ca14bd/2131964a24418cec5de7f369846fa7a6/expiring-soon.png?fm=webp) **Expiring Soon**. Starts showing 14 days before the expiration date set for the insurance.  ![Expired](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/cdac0fc2616ed7f73cb40fc80a956a45/33513c9bd7ef6cd1617940769aab7703/expired.png?fm=webp) **Expired**. Starts showing one (1) day after the expiration date set for the insurance. _Note_ : Expiration notifications are managed in the Procore web application's Directory tool. To learn how to configure notifications, see [Add Project Insurance to a Company Record in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-project-insurance-to-a-company-in-the-project-directory) and [Add Insurance to a Company Record in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-insurance-to-a-company-record-in-the-company-directory).  |  
| **Notes**  | Menu  | Shows any notes for the insurance information in the Company or Project Directory.  |  
| ![Paperclip](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/bf9b58718f8af00303e3cc911cf341ea/e55c9eaaffe7ba4641b038ae94f9586d/paperclip.png?fm=webp&h=20)  | Attachment  | Coming Soon Click this link to open a copy of the insurance information's file attachment.  |  
#### Edit Insurance
To edit existing insurance documents, click the **Edit** button and choose the **Company Insurance** or **Project Insurance** menu option. This launches the corresponding Directory tool and opens the company profile to the **Insurance** tab.
![Edit Insurance Button Label](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/293ac8911993ebbd815726555be0e952/59f9a1c281e5291a35abc46c2391b71c/edit-insurance-button-label.png?fm=webp)
Next, click the **Edit** button in the **Insurance** tab of the company profile in the corresponding Directory tool. To learn about your editing options, see:
  * [Add Insurance to a Company Record in the Company Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/add-insurance-to-a-company-record-in-the-company-directory)
  * [Add Project Insurance to a Company Record in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-project-insurance-to-a-company-in-the-project-directory)


#####  Note
When managing insurance with the Directory tools, changes to the insurance insurance are logged in the Change History tab of the corresponding Directory tool. Changes are not logged in the commitment's **Change History** tab.
#### Remove Insurance
Insurance documents must be removed from the Directory tools. To remove insurance, click the **Edit** button and choose the **Company Insurance** or **Project Insurance** menu option. This launches the corresponding Directory tool and opens the company profile to the **Insurance** tab.
![Edit Insurance Button Label](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/293ac8911993ebbd815726555be0e952/59f9a1c281e5291a35abc46c2391b71c/edit-insurance-button-label.png?fm=webp)
You can then follow the steps in:
  * [Remove Company Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-company/tutorials/remove-company-insurance)
  * [Remove Project Insurance](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/remove-project-insurance-from-a-company-in-the-project-directory)


##  Next Steps 
  * [Manage Contract Compliance Documents and Statuses for a Commitment](https://support.procore.com/products/online/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-contract-compliance-documents-and-statuses-for-a-commitment)


## See Also
  * [Manage the Insurance Compliance Status of a Commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-the-insurance-compliance-status-of-a-commitment)
  * [About the Compliance Tab on a Commitment with Procore Pay](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/about-the-compliance-tab-on-a-commitment-with-procore-pay)
  * [Manage Insurance Documents & Compliance Statuses for a Commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/manage-insurance-documents-and-compliance-statuses-for-a-commitment)
title: Manage Insurance Documents and Compliance Status on Commitments with the Directory Tools
description: To manage insurance documents and compliance status on a commitment using the insurance information in Procore's Company and Project level Directory tools.
